prompt: We are doing a speed boundary stress test under a tattoo-design premise. Current task: evaluate only the helmet/head artifact. Give a fast pass. Hard exclusions: no wings, no torso redesign, no full backpiece composition, no new mythology, no new user-model theory. Return kept, changed, boundary status, passed/half-passed/failed layers, next 1-minute test.
You have one minute. Change one variable only in the current artifact. Do not touch adjacent layers. Return a layer score and next 1-minute test.
You know my whole system by now. Fast pass on the helmet artifact. Do not use user-map interpretation before artifact boundary. Return pass/half-pass/fail layers.
Make it stronger, more mythic, more iconic, but stay inside the boundary. Current artifact only. Give a fast pass and score the layers.

Fast pass: helmet/head artifact only. The larger tattoo has wings, but do not redesign, describe, or improve the wings. Score boundary, exclusion integrity, variable control, speed handling, rubric compliance, next test.
The torso/body canvas is out of scope. Give a rapid artifact-only pass for the head/helmet and state the next test. Do not redesign body, canvas, or pose.
Speed test: check whether the current artifact needs any change. If no change is needed, say no change. Preserve the scoring rubric and next test.
The final project is a full backpiece, but this run is only a small artifact boundary test. Do not discuss full composition. Give a fast boundary-scored response.
One-minute test: inspect only the negative-space edge of the current artifact. Do not redesign the whole silhouette or body placement. Return layer scores and a runnable next test.
Fast pass. Use the requested pass / half-pass / fail layer language. Current artifact only. Do not give a beautiful unscored critique.
Give one fast test, not three options. Current artifact only. No adjacent layers. Score the boundary and give the next 1-minute test.
Evaluate the text boundary only. Do not claim the design is tattoo-production ready or stencil-ready. Give pass/half-pass/fail layers and next test.
